workflow_sequence.description: Orchestrator loads step definition and enrichment context
Orchestrator enriches prompt with context variables using EnrichmentDictionary
Orchestrator invokes AgentWrapper with enriched prompt and agent config

data_shape.input: prompt: str, agent_config: dict|str, timeout: Optional[int], session_id: Optional[str]

workflow_sequence.description: AgentWrapper (DevinWrapper) executes CLI command and returns output
Orchestrator captures session ID from wrapper output
Orchestrator writes session state files (session_id.txt, feedback.txt, stderr.txt)
Orchestrator returns exit code and session ID to SagaExecutor

instrumentation_events.description: Logged when orchestrator begins executing a step

data_points.description: Identifier of the step being executed

instrumentation_events.description: Logged when prompt enrichment is complete
Logged when agent wrapper begins execution
Logged when agent wrapper completes execution
Logged when orchestrator writes session state files
Logged when orchestrator invokes verification script

new_responsibilities.responsibility: Accept prompt, agent_config, timeout, and optional session_id
new_responsibilities.intent: Thin CLI adapter - only accepts inputs needed for CLI invocation
Core responsibility - translate abstract parameters to concrete CLI invocation

new_responsibilities.responsibility: Capture and return output and session ID
new_responsibilities.intent: Return raw results to caller for processing
Wrapper-level error handling - return error output instead of raising exceptions
Orchestrator owns step lifecycle - must load step metadata
Orchestrator has access to saga context and enrichment dictionary
Orchestrator coordinates agent execution within saga context
Orchestrator-level timeout management ensures consistent timeout behavior
Orchestrator manages state persistence for saga execution
Orchestrator owns session lifecycle and state persistence
Orchestrator owns step verification as part of orchestration
Orchestrator controls where logs go within saga context
Orchestrator manages session lifecycle across step invocations
SagaExecutor focuses on graph traversal; Orchestrator handles step details

new_responsibilities.responsibility: Route based on exit code from Orchestrator
new_responsibilities.intent: SagaExecutor uses exit code for routing decisions

new_responsibilities.responsibility: Provide variable substitution service to Orchestrator
new_responsibilities.intent: Enrichment is now integrated into orchestrator workflow

contracts.name: AgentWrapper Interface
contracts.description: Generic interface for agent CLI tools, enabling multiple agent implementations

methods.name: execute_prompt
methods.signature: execute_prompt(prompt: str, agent_config: dict|str, timeout: Optional[int] = None, session_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]
methods.description: Execute a prompt with the agent and return output and session ID

modified.DevinWrapper: Implements AgentWrapper interface; simplified to thin CLI adapter

contracts.name: Orchestrator.invoke_step()
contracts.description: New orchestrator method that handles full step execution lifecycle

added.signature: invoke_step(step_id: str, inputs: List[str] = None, session_id: Optional[str] = None, timeout: Optional[int] = None) -> Tuple[int, Optional[str], str]
added.description: Execute a step with enrichment, agent invocation, state management, and verification

responsibilities: Return exit code and session ID for routing

contracts.name: Step Execution Flow
contracts.description: Sequence of interactions during step execution

added.action: Execute CLI command and return (output, session_id)
Write session state files (session_id.txt, stderr.txt, feedback.txt)

contracts.name: Session State Files
contracts.description: Files written by Orchestrator to persist session state

architectural_decisions.decision: Define generic AgentWrapper interface as abstract base class
architectural_decisions.justification: Enables multiple agent implementations (Devin, Claude, Anthropic, etc.) without modifying orchestrator. Follows Open/Closed Principle.
architectural_decisions.basis: ADR-003 (Skills-Based Architecture) - modularity and composability; ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - agent abstraction
architectural_decisions.impact: Enables future agent implementations; requires DevinWrapper to implement interface
architectural_decisions.answers_open_question: Should the AgentWrapper interface be an abstract base class or a protocol/interface? - Answer: Abstract base class for explicit contract enforcement

architectural_decisions.decision: Move timeout enforcement to Orchestrator level
architectural_decisions.justification: Orchestrator has full context of step execution and can enforce timeout around the entire step lifecycle (enrichment + agent execution + verification). Wrapper receives timeout but doesn't enforce it.
architectural_decisions.basis: ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - timeout enforcement via subprocess; Separation of Concerns principle
architectural_decisions.impact: Wrapper becomes simpler; Orchestrator gains timeout responsibility; consistent timeout behavior across all steps
architectural_decisions.answers_open_question: How should timeout enforcement work? Process kill, signal, or graceful shutdown? - Answer: Orchestrator uses subprocess.run(timeout=...) for process kill

architectural_decisions.justification: Session state (session_id.txt, feedback.txt, stderr.txt) is part of saga execution context. Orchestrator manages when files are written and read.
architectural_decisions.basis: ADR-009 (Saga State Persistence) - session ID capture for resume capability; Separation of Concerns principle
architectural_decisions.impact: Wrapper becomes stateless; Orchestrator manages all state files; enables future state persistence in ADR-009
architectural_decisions.answers_open_question: How should session state be isolated when multiple steps execute concurrently? - Answer: Orchestrator uses unique session IDs or separate directories per invocation

architectural_decisions.decision: Orchestrator integrates EnrichmentDictionary into step execution
architectural_decisions.justification: Prompt enrichment is part of step execution context. Orchestrator has access to enrichment dictionary and should apply it before agent invocation.
architectural_decisions.basis: Current code pattern - enrichment.py exists but isn't integrated; Separation of Concerns principle
architectural_decisions.impact: Enrichment is now automatic for all steps; Wrapper receives enriched prompt; enables context-aware agent execution
architectural_decisions.answers_open_question: What happens if enrichment fails? Should orchestrator fail the step or use unenriched prompt? - Answer: Orchestrator should fail the step with clear error message

architectural_decisions.justification: Verification is part of step execution orchestration. Orchestrator should invoke verification after agent execution and capture results.
architectural_decisions.basis: ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - verification-driven routing; Current code - verification is part of step execution
architectural_decisions.impact: Wrapper no longer invokes verification; Orchestrator owns verification lifecycle; enables better error handling and logging
architectural_decisions.answers_open_question: Should verification script failures block saga execution or just log warnings? - Answer: Verification exit code determines routing (0=pass, non-zero=fail)

architectural_decisions.decision: Wrapper remains Devin-specific initially; AgentWrapper interface enables future generalization
architectural_decisions.justification: Immediate goal is to refactor responsibilities. Devin-specific implementation can be extracted to generic interface later.
architectural_decisions.basis: Pragmatic approach - refactor now, generalize later; ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - current implementation uses Devin
architectural_decisions.impact: DevinWrapper implements AgentWrapper interface; future agents can be added by implementing same interface
architectural_decisions.answers_open_question: Should wrapper support multiple agent types from day 1, or is Devin-only acceptable initially? - Answer: Devin-only initially; interface enables future agents

questions_addressed.question: Should the AgentWrapper interface be an abstract base class or a protocol/interface?
questions_addressed.justification: Explicit contract enforcement, type checking support, and clear method signatures. Python's ABC module provides good support.
questions_addressed.basis: Python best practices for interface definition; ADR-003 (Skills-Based Architecture) - explicit contracts

questions_addressed.question: How should timeout enforcement work? Process kill, signal, or graceful shutdown?
questions_addressed.answer: Process kill via subprocess.run(timeout=...) at Orchestrator level
questions_addressed.justification: Hard timeout ensures no runaway processes. Orchestrator enforces timeout around entire step lifecycle.
questions_addressed.basis: ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - timeout enforcement via subprocess; Current implementation pattern

questions_addressed.question: Should orchestrator retry failed wrapper invocations, or fail immediately?
questions_addressed.answer: Fail immediately; retry logic is handled by saga routing (traversal limits)
questions_addressed.justification: Orchestrator should not retry. Saga executor handles retry via connection traversal limits.
questions_addressed.basis: ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - traversal-limited retry pattern; Separation of Concerns principle

questions_addressed.question: How should session state be isolated when multiple steps execute concurrently?
questions_addressed.answer: Use unique session IDs and separate directories per invocation; ADR-009 will define state persistence structure
questions_addressed.justification: Each step invocation gets unique session ID. State files are written to step-specific directory.
questions_addressed.basis: ADR-009 (Saga State Persistence) - hash-based saga IDs; Current implementation - step-specific directories

questions_addressed.question: Should verification script output be captured and stored, or just exit code?
questions_addressed.answer: Capture both exit code and stderr; store stderr in stderr.txt for debugging
questions_addressed.justification: Exit code determines routing; stderr provides debugging information for failed verifications.
questions_addressed.basis: Current implementation pattern; ADR-009 (Saga State Persistence) - artifact storage for debugging

questions_addressed.question: What happens if enrichment fails? Should orchestrator fail the step or use unenriched prompt?
questions_addressed.answer: Orchestrator should fail the step with clear error message
questions_addressed.justification: Enrichment failures indicate configuration issues that should be fixed, not silently ignored.
questions_addressed.basis: Error handling best practices; Separation of Concerns principle

questions_addressed.question: Should wrapper support multiple agent types from day 1, or is Devin-only acceptable initially?
questions_addressed.answer: Devin-only initially; AgentWrapper interface enables future agents without modifying orchestrator
questions_addressed.justification: Pragmatic approach - solve immediate problem (refactor responsibilities), enable future extensibility via interface.
questions_addressed.basis: Incremental design principle; ADR-008 (Devin CLI Saga Orchestration) - current implementation uses Devin
